findings.summary: runPathsPing/runSvtnStatus hardcoded useJSON=true — always emitted the {"ok":true,"data":...} envelope, --json a silent no-op; violates interface-definitions v1.31 §214, diverges from house pattern (paths list/router status/list-keys/this story's own reload+drain), AC-001 PC-4 + AC-005 PC-1 bare literals never produced top-level.
findings.escalation_record: The same observation was classified NITPICK by three consecutive fresh passes (N-CS-I1-01, N-CS-I2-02, N-CS-I3-01) and orchestrator-sanctioned each time on their shared "no AC mandates --json" reasoning. Pass 4 located the governing §214 clause OUTSIDE the ACs — the prior sanctions are superseded, the finding is accepted, and the code freeze is LIFTED. Recorded as multi-pass-discipline evidence for BC-5.39.001.
findings.remediation: TDD-shaped. RED dfd51bc (test-writer): 3 happy-path tests split into default_bare_data/json_flag_envelope subtests, shared helpers, verified FAILING against the always-envelope implementation; confirmed no AC mandates the envelope in default mode; AC-005 PC-1 also specifies a bare literal. GREEN 100d28890eb7a07541aa9aa93be8339faa8b5e4d (implementer): useJSON threaded through every writeError/writeSuccess/connectAndRun call in both functions; both //nolint:unparam annotations dropped; no new lint issues.

findings.summary: client-side missing-flag paths double-emitted writeError(true,"E-CFG-001",...) + reported(usageErrf(...)) — the canonical pattern per Ruling 2 Addendum + the §110/§111 siblings is a bare usageErrf.
findings.remediation: Fixed in 100d288: bare usageErrf, message text byte-identical, shape-agnostic missing-flag tests stayed green.
